glossary.definition: A business registered on AnjiSchedulo. Each tenant is fully isolated — their data, configuration, and events are never visible to other tenants.
A confirmed, scheduled reservation between a customer and a staff member for a specific service at a specific time. An appointment is the core aggregate of the system.
The act of a customer requesting and completing an appointment. Booking is the process; Appointment is the result.
A specific time window on a specific date for a specific staff member during which they are available to perform a service.
A database-level exclusive reservation placed on a slot when a booking is in progress. Prevents two customers from confirming the same slot concurrently.
The computed set of open slots for a staff member on a given date, derived from their working hours, existing confirmed appointments, and manual blocks.
A bookable offering defined by a tenant. Has a name, duration, and price. Examples: Haircut (45 min, £25), Initial Consultation (60 min, £80).
A manual period of unavailability set by a staff member or admin, outside of regular working hours. Prevents bookings during that period.
A tenant-configured rule defining how many hours before an appointment a customer is permitted to cancel. Defined per tenant in tenant_config.
The choreographed sequence of events triggered when an appointment is cancelled: refund → slot release → notifications. Each step is independent.
The orchestrated sequence of steps required to confirm a booking: reserve slot → charge payment → write confirmed event. Fails atomically with compensation.
The pattern by which an appointment's current state is derived by replaying its ordered event history (requested → confirmed → rescheduled → completed) rather than reading a status column.
A database table written in the same transaction as an event. The Relay Service polls the outbox and publishes events to the message bus, guaranteeing at-least-once delivery without dual-write risk.
A queue that receives events which have failed processing after all retry attempts. Monitored by the Ops Service; contents are triaged and re-queued or discarded by a Platform Operator.
The act of re-publishing all historical events for a tenant in order, allowing downstream services (dashboard, analytics, query views) to rebuild their state. Used after bug fixes or data migrations.
A pre-aggregated read-optimised data structure (e.g. tenant_dashboard_view) updated incrementally per event, enabling O(1) reads regardless of booking history depth.
The runtime object carrying tenant_id, user_id, role, and correlation_id through a request or event processing chain. Always derived from the JWT or event envelope — never from the request body.
A PostgreSQL feature enforced at the database layer. Ensures queries only return rows belonging to the current tenant, acting as a safety net even if application code omits a tenant_id filter.
A UUID generated at the API Gateway and attached to every HTTP header, log line, and event envelope in a request chain. Enables full distributed tracing across services.
A message consumer that produces the same outcome regardless of how many times it processes the same message. Implemented via inbox deduplication or event_id watermarks.
